debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
C : Category α
X Y : α
f : Hom X Y
hf : IsIso f
⊢ @CategoryInContext.Category.Mono α C X Y f ∧ @CategoryInContext.Category.Epi α C X Y f

failed_attempts: refine' ⟨invertibleToMono f hf, invertibleToEpi f hf⟩

have h₁ : Mono f := by
    -- Use the fact that an isomorphism is a monomorphism
    have h₂ : IsMono f := hf.toMono
    exact h₂
  have h₂ : Epi f := by
    -- Use the fact that an isomorphism is an epimorphism
    have h₃ : IsEpi f := hf.toEpi
    exact h₃
  exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

have h_mono : Mono f := by sorry
  have h_epi : Epi f := by sorry
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩

have h1 : Mono f := by
    have h2 : IsMono f := by
      obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := hf
      exact hg.1
    intro W h k hcomp
    have h3 : g.exists_right_inv h := by
      have h4 : g ≫ f = id X := by
        rw [hg.2]
        <;> simp [id_comp]
      have h5 : g ≫ f = id X := by
        rw [hg.2]
        <;> simp [id_comp]
      have h6 : g.exists_right_inv h := by
        classical
          have h7 : g.exists_right_inv h := by
            exact ⟨h, by simp_all [h4]⟩
          exact h7
      exact h6
    have h8 : g.exists_right_inv k := by
      have h9 : g ≫ f = id X := by
        rw [hg.2]
        <;> simp [id_comp]
      have h10 : g.exists_right_inv k := by
        classical
          have h11 : g.exists_right_inv k := by
            exact ⟨k, by simp_all [h9]⟩
          exact h11
      exact h10
    have h12 : g.exists_right_inv h := h3
    have h13 : g.exists_right_inv k := h8
    have h14 : h = k := by
      have h15 : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := hcomp
      have h16 : h = k := by
        apply h2
        <;> simp_all [hcomp]
      exact h16
    exact h14
  have h2 : Epi f := by
    have h3 : IsEpi f := by
      obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := hf
      exact hg.2
    intro Z h k hcomp
    have h4 : g.exists_left_inv h := by
      have h5 : f ≫ g = id Y := by
        rw [hg.1]
        <;> simp [comp_id]
      have h6 : g.exists_left_inv h := by
        classical
          have h7 : g.exists_left_inv h := by
            exact ⟨h, by simp_all [h5]⟩
          exact h7
      exact h6
    have h7 : g.exists_left_inv k := by
      have h8 : f ≫ g = id Y := by
        rw [hg.1]
        <;> simp [comp_id]
      have h9 : g.exists_left_inv k := by
        classical
          have h10 : g.exists_left_inv k := by
            exact ⟨k, by simp_all [h8]⟩
          exact h10
      exact h9
    have h10 : g.exists_left_inv h := h4
    have h11 : g.exists_left_inv k := h7
    have h12 : h = k := by
      have h13 : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := hcomp
      have h14 : h = k := by
        apply h3
        <;> simp_all [hcomp]
      exact h14
    exact h12
  exact ⟨h1, h2⟩

have h_mono : Mono f := by sorry
  have h_epi : Epi f := by sorry
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩

refine' ⟨fun W h₁ h₂ h₃ => by
    have h₄ := hf.isEquiv.left_inv h₁
    have h₅ := hf.isEquiv.left_inv h₂
    have h₆ : h₁ ≫ f = h₂ ≫ f := h₃
    have h₇ : h₁ = h₂ := by
      calc
        h₁ = h₁ ≫ f ≫ hf.isEquiv.right_inv := by
          simp [h₄]
        _ = h₂ ≫ f ≫ hf.isEquiv.right_inv := by rw [h₆]
        _ = h₂ ≫ (f ≫ hf.isEquiv.right_inv) := by rfl
        _ = h₂ ≫ 1 := by simp [hf.isEquiv.right_inv]
        _ = h₂ := by simp
    exact h₇
  , fun Z h₁ h₂ h₃ => by
    have h₄ := hf.isEquiv.left_inv h₁
    have h₅ := hf.isEquiv.left_inv h₂
    have h₆ : f ≫ h₁ = f ≫ h₂ := h₃
    have h₇ : h₁ = h₂ := by
      calc
        h₁ = hf.isEquiv.right_inv ≫ (f ≫ h₁) := by simp [hf.isEquiv.right_inv]
        _ = hf.isEquiv.right_inv ≫ (f ≫ h₂) := by rw [h₆]
        _ = (hf.isEquiv.right_inv ≫ f) ≫ h₂ := by rfl
        _ = 1 ≫ h₂ := by simp [hf.isEquiv.right_inv]
        _ = h₂ := by simp
    exact h₇⟩

have h_mono : @Monomorphism α C X Y f := by
    have : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), IsInverse f g := by
      rcases hf with ⟨g, hg⟩
      exact ⟨g, hg⟩
    obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := this
    have hGF : g ≫ f = 1 := hg.left
    intro {W} h₁ h₂ h₁₂
    have h₁₂' : h₁ ≫ f = h₂ ≫ f := h₁₂
    calc
      h₁ = h₁ ≫ 1 := by simp
      _ = h₁ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [hGF]
      _ = (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [← assoc]
      _ = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by
        have h₁₂'' : (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by
          calc
            (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = h₁ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [← assoc]
            _ = h₂ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by
              congr 1
              rw [← attackerComputer;
                simp_all [Monomorphism]
              ]
            _ = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [← assoc]
        rw [h₁₂'']
      _ = h₂ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [← assoc]
      _ = h₂ ≫ 1 := by rw [hGF]
      _ = h₂ := by simp
  have h_epi : @Epimorphism α C X Y f := by
    have : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), IsInverse f g := by
      rcases hf with ⟨g, hg⟩
      exact ⟨g, hg⟩
    obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := this
    have h_fg : f ≫ g = 1 := hg.right
    intro {Z} h₁ h₂ h₁₂
    have h₁₂' : f ≫ h₁ = f ≫ h₂ := h₁₂
    calc
      h₁ = 1 ≫ h₁ := by simp
      _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₁ := by rw [h_fg]
      _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₁) := by rw [← assoc]
      _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₂) := by
        have h₁₂'' : f ≫ (g ≫ h₁) = f ≫ (g ≫ h₂) := by
          calc
            f ≫ (g ≫ h₁) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₁ := by rw [← assoc]
            _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₂ := by
              congr 1
              rw [← attackerComputer;
                simp_all [Epimorphism]
              ]
            _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₂) := by rw [← assoc]
        rw [h₁₂'']
      _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₂ := by rw [← assoc]
      _ = 1 ≫ h₂ := by rw [h_fg]
      _ = h₂ := by simp
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩

have h_mono : Mono f := hf.mono
  have h_epi : Epi f := hf.epi
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩

have h₁ : Mono f := by
    have h₂ : IsIso f := hf
    -- An isomorphism is monic because it has a left inverse.
    have h₃ : ∀ {W} (h k : Hom W X), h ≫ f = k ≫ f → h = k := by
      intro W h k h_eq
      have h₄ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), g ≫ f = id X := by
        -- Since f is an isomorphism, it has a right inverse.
        obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := hf.symm
        refine' ⟨g, _⟩
        simp_all [IsIso.inv_def]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₄
      calc
        h = h ≫ (id X) := by simp
        _ = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [hg]
        _ = (h ≫ g) ≫ f := by simp [assoc]
        _ = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by
          -- Since h ≫ f = k ≫ f, and g is a right inverse, we can use this to show h ≫ g = k ≫ g.
          have h₅ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h_eq
          have h₆ : (h ≫ g) ≫ f = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by
            calc
              (h ≫ g) ≫ f = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
              _ = h ≫ (id X) := by rw [hg]
              _ = h := by simp
              _ = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by
                simp [hg]
              _ = h ≫ (id X) := by rw [hg]
              _ = h := by simp
              _ = k ≫ (g ≫ f) := by
                have h₇ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h_eq
                have h₈ : (h ≫ g) ≫ f = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by
                  calc
                    (h ≫ g) ≫ f = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
                    _ = h ≫ (id X) := by rw [hg]
                    _ = h := by simp
                    _ = k := by
                      have h₉ : IsIso f := hf
                      have h₁₀ : IsMono f := by
                        -- Since f is an isomorphism, it is monic.
                        exact isIso_iff_isMonicAndIsEpic.mp hf |>.1
                      have h₁₁ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h_eq
                      have h₁₂ : h = k := h₁₀ h₁₁
                      simp_all
                    _ = k ≫ (id X) := by simp
                    _ = k ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [hg]
                    _ = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by simp [assoc]
                simp_all [assoc]
              _ = k ≫ (id X) := by rw [hg]
              _ = k := by simp
          simp_all [assoc]
        _ = k ≫ (g ≫ f) := by simp [assoc]
        _ = k ≫ (id X) := by rw [hg]
        _ = k := by simp
    exact h₃
  have h₂ : Epi f := by
    have h₃ : IsIso f := hf
    -- An isomorphism is epic because it has a right inverse.
    have h₄ : ∀ {Z} (h k : Hom Y Z), f ≫ h = f ≫ k → h = k := by
      intro Z h k h_eq
      have h₅ : ∃ (g : Hom X Y), f ≫ g = id Y := by
        -- Since f is an isomorphism, it has a left inverse.
        obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := hf
        refine' ⟨g, _⟩
        simp_all [IsIso.inv_def]
        <;> aesop
      obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h₅
      calc
        h = (id Y) ≫ h := by simp
        _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [hg]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h) := by simp [assoc]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by
          -- Since f ≫ h = f ≫ k, and g is a left inverse, we can use this to show g ≫ h = g ≫ k.
          have h₆ : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := h_eq
          have h₇ : f ≫ (g ≫ h) = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by
            calc
              f ≫ (g ≫ h) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by simp [assoc]
              _ = (id Y) ≫ h := by rw [hg]
              _ = h := by simp
              _ = f ≫ g ≫ k := by
                have h₈ : IsIso f := hf
                have h₉ : IsEpi f := by
                  -- Since f is an isomorphism, it is epic.
                  exact isIso_iff_isMonicAndIsEpic.mp hf |>.2
                have h₁₀ : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := h_eq
                have h₁₁ : h = k := h₉ h₁₀
                simp_all
              _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by simp [assoc]
              _ = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by simp [assoc]
          simp_all [assoc]
        _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by simp [assoc]
        _ = (id Y) ≫ k := by rw [hg]
        _ = k := by simp
    exact h₄
  exact ⟨h₁, h₂⟩

have h : IsIso f := hf
  have h₁ : Inv f := by exact ⟨h.inv, h.right_inv, h.left_inv⟩
  refine' ⟨_, _⟩
  · -- Prove that f is a monomorphism
    intro W hW k hk
    have h₂ : hW ≫ f = k ≫ f := hk
    have h₃ : hW = k := by
      calc
        hW = hW ≫ (1 : Hom Y Y) := by simp
        _ = hW ≫ (f ≫ h.inv) := by rw [← h.right_inv]
        _ = (hW ≫ f) ≫ h.inv := by simp [assoc]
        _ = (k ≫ f) ≫ h.inv := by rw [h₂]
        _ = k ≫ (f ≫ h.inv) := by simp [assoc]
        _ = k ≫ (1 : Hom Y Y) := by rw [← h.right_inv]
        _ = k := by simp
    exact h₃
  · -- Prove that f is an epimorphism
    intro Z hZ k hk
    have h₂ : f ≫ hZ = f ≫ k := hk
    have h₃ : hZ = k := by
      calc
        hZ = (1 : Hom X X) ≫ hZ := by simp
        _ = (h.inv ≫ f) ≫ hZ := by rw [← h.left_inv]
        _ = h.inv ≫ (f ≫ hZ) := by simp [assoc]
        _ = h.inv ≫ (f ≫ k) := by rw [h₂]
        _ = (h.inv ≫ f) ≫ k := by simp [assoc]
        _ = (1 : Hom X X) ≫ k := by rw [h.left_inv]
        _ = k := by simp
    exact h₃

have h_mono : Mono f := by
    classical
    rcases hf with ⟨g, hg⟩
    intro W h₁ h₂ h₁f_eq_h₂f
    have h₁_eq_h₂ : h₁ = h₂ := by
      calc
        h₁ = h₁ ≫ id X := by simp
        _ = h₁ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by
          rw [← hg.1]
          <;> simp [id_comp]
        _ = (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f := by
          simp [comp_assoc]
        _ = (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f := by rfl
        _ = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by
          congr 1
          calc
            h₁ ≫ g = h₁ ≫ g := by rfl
            _ = h₂ ≫ g := by
              have h₃ : h₁ ≫ f = h₂ ≫ f := h₁f_eq_h₂f
              have h₄ : (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by
                calc
                  (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = h₁ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by simp [comp_assoc]
                  _ = h₁ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rfl
                  _ = h₂ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by
                    have h₅ : h₁ ≫ f = h₂ ≫ f := h₁f_eq_h₂f
                    rw [← comp_assoc]
                    simp_all [comp_assoc]
                    <;> aesop
                  _ = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := by simp [comp_assoc]
              have h₅ : (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := h₄
              have h₆ : (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f = (h₁ ≫ g) ≫ f := rfl
              have h₇ : (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f = (h₂ ≫ g) ≫ f := rfl
              simp_all [comp_assoc]
              <;> aesop
            _ = h₂ ≫ g := by rfl
        _ = h₂ ≫ (g ≫ f) := by simp [comp_assoc]
        _ = h₂ ≫ id X := by rw [hg.1] <;> simp [comp_id]
        _ = h₂ := by simp
    exact h₁_eq_h₂
  
  have h_epi : Epi f := by
    classical
    rcases hf with ⟨g, hg⟩
    intro Z h₁ h₂ h₁f_eq_h₂f
    have h₁_eq_h₂ : h₁ = h₂ := by
      calc
        h₁ = id Y ≫ h₁ := by simp
        _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₁ := by
          rw [← hg.2]
          <;> simp [comp_id]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₁) := by
          simp [comp_assoc]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₁) := by rfl
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h₂) := by
          congr 1
          calc
            g ≫ h₁ = g ≫ h₁ := by rfl
            _ = g ≫ h₂ := by
              have h₃ : h₁ ≫ f = h₂ ≫ f := by
                calc
                  h₁ ≫ f = h₁ ≫ f := by rfl
                  _ = h₂ ≫ f := by
                    rw [h₁f_eq_h₂f]
                    <;> simp_all [comp_assoc]
                    <;> aesop
              have h₄ : (g ≫ h₁) ≫ f = (g ≫ h₂) ≫ f := by
                calc
                  (g ≫ h₁) ≫ f = g ≫ (h₁ ≫ f) := by simp [comp_assoc]
                  _ = g ≫ (h₂ ≫ f) := by rw [h₃]
                  _ = (g ≫ h₂) ≫ f := by simp [comp_assoc]
              have h₅ : (g ≫ h₁) ≫ f = (g ≫ h₂) ≫ f := h₄
              have h₆ : (g ≫ h₁) ≫ f = (g ≫ h₁) ≫ f := rfl
              have h₇ : (g ≫ h₂) ≫ f = (g ≫ h₂) ≫ f := rfl
              simp_all [comp_assoc]
              <;> aesop
            _ = g ≫ h₂ := by rfl
        _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h₂ := by simp [comp_assoc]
        _ = id Y ≫ h₂ := by
          rw [hg.2]
          <;> simp [id_comp]
        _ = h₂ := by simp
    exact h₁_eq_h₂
  
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩

refine' ⟨_, _⟩
  · -- Prove that f is mono
    have h1 : ∀ {W} (h k : Hom W X), h ≫ f = k ≫ f → h = k := by
      intro W h k h_eq
      have h2 : IsIso f := hf
      have h3 : Function.Bijective (fun g : Hom W X => g ≫ f) := by
        have h4 : IsIso f := hf
        exact h4.mul_bijective _
      have h5 : Function.Injective (fun g : Hom W X => g ≫ f) := h3.1
      have h6 : (fun g : Hom W X => g ≫ f) h = (fun g : Hom W X => g ≫ f) k := by
        rw [h_eq]
      exact h5 h6
    exact h1
  · -- Prove that f is epi
    have h1 : ∀ {Z} (h k : Hom Y Z), f ≫ h = f ≫ k → h = k := by
      intro Z h k h_eq
      have h2 : IsIso f := hf
      have h3 : Function.Bijective (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) := by
        have h4 : IsIso f := hf
        exact h4.mul_bijective _
      have h5 : Function.Injective (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) := h3.1
      have h6 : (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) h = (fun g : Hom Y Z => f ≫ g) k := by
        rw [h_eq]
      exact h5 h6
    exact h1

classical
  have h₁ : ∀ {W} (h k : Hom W X), h ≫ f = k ≫ f → h = k := by
    -- Given that f is an isomorphism, we can use the inverse of f to show that if h ≫ f = k ≫ f, then h = k.
    have h₂ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), f ≫ g = id Y ∧ g ≫ f = id X := hf
    obtain ⟨g, hg₁, hg₂⟩ := h₂
    intro W h k h₃
    have h₄ : h = k := by
      calc
        h = h ≫ id X := by simp
        _ = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [hg₂]
        _ = (h ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [assoc]
        _ = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by
          have h₅ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h₃
          have h₆ : (h ≫ g) ≫ f = (h ≫ g) ≫ f := rfl
          calc
            (h ≫ g) ≫ f = h ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [← assoc]
            _ = h ≫ id X := by rw [hg₂]
            _ = h := by simp
            _ = k ≫ id X := by
              have h₇ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h₃
              have h₈ : k ≫ id X = k := by simp
              have h₉ : h ≫ id X = h := by simp
              have h₁₀ : h ≫ f = k ≫ f := h₃
              have h₁₁ : h ≫ (g ≫ f) = (h ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [assoc]
              simp_all [hg₂, h₉, h₈]
              <;>
              (try simp_all [assoc]) <;>
              (try simp_all [hf]) <;>
              (try simp_all [hg₁, hg₂]) <;>
              (try simp_all [h₃])
            _ = k ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [hg₂]
            _ = (k ≫ g) ≫ f := by rw [← assoc]
        _ = k ≫ (g ≫ f) := by rw [← assoc]
        _ = k ≫ id X := by rw [hg₂]
        _ = k := by simp
    exact h₄
  -- Use the fact that h ≫ f = k ≫ f implies h = k to show that f is a monomorphism.
  have h₂ : ∀ {Z} (h k : Hom Y Z), f ≫ h = f ≫ k → h = k := by
    -- Similarly, we can use the inverse of f to show that if f ≫ h = f ≫ k, then h = k.
    have h₃ : ∃ (g : Hom Y X), f ≫ g = id Y ∧ g ≫ f = id X := hf
    obtain ⟨g, hg₁, hg₂⟩ := h₃
    intro Z h k h₄
    have h₅ : h = k := by
      calc
        h = id Y ≫ h := by simp
        _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [hg₁]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h) := by rw [← assoc]
        _ = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by
          have h₆ : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := h₄
          have h₇ : f ≫ (g ≫ h) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [← assoc]
          have h₈ : f ≫ (g ≫ k) = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by rw [← assoc]
          calc
            f ≫ (g ≫ h) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [← assoc]
            _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by
              have h₉ : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := h₄
              have h₁₀ : (f ≫ g) ≫ h = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by
                calc
                  (f ≫ g) ≫ h = f ≫ (g ≫ h) := by rw [← assoc]
                  _ = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by
                    have h₁₁ : g ≫ h = g ≫ k := by
                      have h₁₂ : h = k := by
                        calc
                          h = id Y ≫ h := by simp
                          _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [hg₁]
                          _ = f ≫ (g ≫ h) := by rw [← assoc]
                          _ = f ≫ (g ≫ k) := by
                            have h₁₃ : f ≫ h = f ≫ k := h₄
                            have h₁₄ : f ≫ (g ≫ h) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h := by rw [← assoc]
                            have h₁₅ : f ≫ (g ≫ k) = (f ≫ g) ≫ k := by rw [← assoc]
                            calc

              have h₄ : g ≫ (f ≫ hZ) = g ≫ (f ≫ kZ) := by rw [h₃]
              calc
                g ≫ hZ = (g ≫ (f ≫ g)) ≫ hZ := by
                  simp [hg₂, Associative]
                  <;>
                  simp_all [Associative]
                  <;>
                  try simp_all [Associative]
                  <;>
                  try simp_all [Associative]
                _ = g ≫ (f ≫ g) ≫ hZ := by simp [Associative]
                _ = g ≫ (f ≫ (g ≫ hZ)) := by simp [Associative]
                _ = g ≫ (f ≫ (g ≫ kZ)) := by
                  have h₅ : f ≫ (g ≫ hZ) = f ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by
                    calc
                      f ≫ (g ≫ hZ) = (f ≫ g) ≫ hZ := by simp [Associative]
                      _ = 1 ≫ hZ := by rw [hg₂]
                      _ = hZ := by simp
                      _ = kZ := by
                        calc
                          hZ = (f ≫ g) ≫ hZ := by simp [hg₂]
                          _ = f ≫ (g ≫ hZ) := by simp [Associative]
                          _ = f ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by
                            have h₆ : g ≫ hZ = g ≫ kZ := by
                              calc
                                g ≫ hZ = g ≫ hZ := rfl
                                _ = g ≫ kZ := by
                                  have h₇ : f ≫ hZ = f ≫ kZ := hfZ
                                  have h₈ : g ≫ (f ≫ hZ) = g ≫ (f ≫ kZ) := by rw [h₇]
                                  simp_all [Associative]
                                  <;>
                                  try simp_all [Associative]
                                  <;>
                                  try simp_all [Associative]
                            rw [h₆]
                          _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ kZ := by simp [Associative]
                          _ = 1 ≫ kZ := by rw [hg₂]
                          _ = kZ := by simp
                      _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ kZ := by simp [hg₂]
                      _ = f ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by simp [Associative]
                  simp_all [Associative]
                  <;>
                  try simp_all [Associative]
                  <;>
                  try simp_all [Associative]
                _ = g ≫ (f ≫ (g ≫ kZ)) := by rfl
                _ = (g ≫ f) ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by simp [Associative]
                _ = 1 ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by rw [hg₁]
                _ = g ≫ kZ := by simp
          <;> simp_all [Associative]
          <;> try simp_all [Associative]
          <;> try simp_all [Associative]
        rw [h₂]
      _ = f ≫ (g ≫ kZ) := by rfl
      _ = (f ≫ g) ≫ kZ := by simp [Associative]
      _ = 1 ≫ kZ := by rw [hg₂]
      _ = kZ := by simp
  
  exact ⟨h_mono, h_epi⟩
